question: how can i fix early timing?
content:
Early timing means the swing arrives too soon.
At the foul line:
Your slide foot is not fully planted yet, but the ball is already dropping past parallel
This forces you to grab, pull, slow the arm, or lose balance.
Key rule:
You never fix early timing by slowing your arm.
You fix it by delaying when the ball arrives at the foul line.

The 5 correct fixes (choose 1–2, or a small mix of them all)
1. Move closer to the foul line (best fix)
Stand 30–40 cm closer than normal.
Why it works
Shorter approach = less time for the swing to arrive early
Timing improves instantly without touching the swing
This is the most reliable and universal correction.

2. Delay the pushaway slightly
Start the pushaway later relative to your first step.
Effect
The ball begins falling later
The whole swing shifts back in time
Very effective if you rush the start
Do NOT slow the arm, just start it later

3. Raise the starting ball position slightly
*Hold the ball a bit higher at setup.
Why
Higher start = longer swing arc
Longer arc = more time before arrival
Small change, big timing effect
*Only a few centimeters — don’t exaggerate

4. Allow a naturally longer swing
Do not force height, just stop cutting it short.
Why
Short swings arrive early
Longer pendulum = delayed arrival
Especially useful for bowlers with compact swings

5. Adjust step speed (advanced)
Faster steps will also correct the timing, allowing you to reach the foul line sooner than you do now.
What NOT to do (this makes early timing worse)
❌ Slow your arm
❌ Pull the ball down
❌ Force loft
❌ Grab at release
All of these replace gravity with muscle and destroy consistency.
Best drill to retrain timing
Reverse drills
1 step
2 step
3 step
These teach your body when the ball should fall relative to the feet, without approach complexity.
